---
title: "Introduction to gpr: General Power for R"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Introduction to gpr: General Power for R}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

## Overview

gpr is a comprehensive power analysis package for R implementing statistical 
power analyses for t-tests, F-tests, chi-square tests, z-tests, and exact tests.

## Installation
```r
pak::pak("Stevejb1/gpr")
```

## Basic Usage

### A priori: How many participants do I need?
```r
library(gpr)

gpr_ttest(test = "two.sample", analysis = "a_priori",
          effect_size = 0.5, alpha = 0.05, power = 0.80)
```

### Post hoc: What power did I achieve?
```r
gpr_ttest(test = "two.sample", analysis = "post_hoc",
          effect_size = 0.5, alpha = 0.05, n = 50)
```

### F-test: One-way ANOVA
```r
gpr_ftest(test = "anova.one.way", analysis = "a_priori",
          effect_size = 0.25, alpha = 0.05, power = 0.80,
          groups = 3)
```

### Chi-square: Goodness of fit
```r
gpr_chisq(test = "gof", analysis = "a_priori",
          effect_size = 0.30, alpha = 0.05, power = 0.80,
          df = 5)
```

### Effect size converters
```r
gpr_effect_size(0.5, from = "d")
effect_size_benchmarks()
```

## Interfaces

### Shiny GUI
```r
launch_gpr()
```

### Console menu
```r
gpr()
```

## Citation

Faul, F., Erdfelder, E., Lang, A.-G., & Buchner, A. (2007). G*Power 3: 
A flexible statistical power analysis program for the social, behavioral, 
and biomedical sciences. *Behavior Research Methods, 39*, 175-191.
